title: Grant McLean - So You Want To Be A Developer? WITcon 2018
text: A concise and casual talk with Grant McLean which introduces interesting concepts with a very warm and welcoming approach. His discussion of Type F and J programmers has resonated with me deeply, inspiring one of my own recent talks. I've enjoyed sharing his principle with many others.

title: "Software Fundamentals Matter More Than Ever" — Matt Pocock
text: In an uncertain time where people are unsure what to learn (or if to continue learning in this field at all), Matt brings us back to the grounding foundations. What began as important, and what will remain to be important.

title: Laid Off After 25 Years in Tech: The Anxiety, Sacrifice, and Reality No One Talks About
text: An insightful talk that reinstates the importance of constant curiosity and adaption, treasuring your moments outside of work, and remembering that corporations are ultimately just corportations.

title: The Extra Load We Carry
text: Annie (the writer of this article) spoke at my work last month, and I was engaged at every sentence. She introduced 2 awesome concepts to me: Self-Efficacy and Knowledge Debt, the use of AI needs to consider these always. This article is affirming to the common emotions that women in technology experience.
